purpose: Patterns and grammatical-gender cues for certifying a noun's plural form and its gender BEFORE authoring a scripture-facing gloss or an agreement note. Consumed by the noun-plural-gender procedure. A plural pattern observed on the surface PROPOSES a class; it never certifies the singular root by itself — defer root certification to ../rules/root-decision-rules.json. Gender is a property of the lemma, not of the surface tā' marbūṭa: many feminine words carry no ـة and several ـة-bearing words are masculine. Public artifacts stay {src:'qamus', kind:'authored', lang:'en'}; QAC POS/gender is INTERNAL evidence only and is never exposed.

fields.kind: msl (jamʿ mudhakkar sālim) | mfl (jamʿ muʾannath sālim) | taksir (jamʿ taksīr) | cue
fields.wazn: the template, written with ف ع ل radical placeholders and full diacritics
fields.test: the operational signal on the diacritized surface (which letters / harakāt / suffix)
fields.singular_hint: how the singular relates to the plural; never a certification on its own
fields.gender: grammatical gender the pattern implies, or 'lemma-bound' when the pattern is gender-neutral
fields.example_loc: a quran:S:A:W where the form occurs
fields.guard: what must hold; what NOT to infer from this pattern

decide_on_diacritics: Every test reads the FULL diacritized surface. A bare skeleton (مسلمون vs no harakāt) cannot distinguish masculine-sound nominative مُسْلِمُونَ from a hollow stem; if the harakāt or the case-suffix vowel is absent, emit pending rather than guessing the commoner reading. The case ending (ـُونَ/ـِينَ for msl, ـَاتٌ/ـَاتٍ for mfl) is part of the signal.

patterns.test: stem + ـُونَ (wāw-ḍamma + nūn-fatḥa) in the nominative; suffix ـِينَ (yā'-kasra + nūn) in the accusative/genitive.
patterns.singular_hint: drop the case suffix → the masculine singular (مُسْلِمٌ ← مُسْلِمُونَ). The suffix is inflection, never a radical.
patterns.guard: Sound-masculine plural is reserved for rational/animate masculine referents and a small fixed set (سِنُونَ, أَرَضُونَ). Do not treat the ـُونَ/ـِينَ as part of the root; do not certify a root from the suffix. Distinguish from the dual ـَانِ/ـَيْنِ.

patterns.test: stem + ـِينَ (yā'-kasra + nūn-fatḥa). Same lemma as msl-nominative, oblique case.
patterns.singular_hint: identical singular to msl-nominative; only the case vowel differs.
patterns.guard: Do NOT confuse the oblique sound-masculine ـِينَ with the broken-plural feminine ـِينَ-shaped endings or with the dual oblique ـَيْنِ. The yā' here carries kasra and the nūn fatḥa.

patterns.test: stem + ـَاتٌ (alif + tā' maftūḥa, NOT tā' marbūṭa) with ḍamma in the nominative, kasra (ـَاتٍ) in the oblique.
patterns.singular_hint: the singular usually ends in tā' marbūṭa (مُسْلِمَةٌ ← مُسْلِمَاتٌ); the marbūṭa opens to a maftūḥa tā' in the plural.
patterns.guard: The oblique sound-feminine takes KASRA not fatḥa (مُسْلِمَاتٍ, not *مُسْلِمَاتَ) — it is a diptote-exception that takes kasra. Do not read the final ـَات as a feminine of the broken pattern فَعَلَات without checking soundness.

patterns.test: prefixed hamza-fatḥa + skeletal فعل; common plural of triliteral nouns.
patterns.singular_hint: singular is often فَعْل/فِعْل (قَوْل ← أَقْوَال, نَهْر ← أَنْهَار). The internal vowel change IS the plural — no sound suffix.
patterns.guard: Broken plurals are gender-neutral on the surface: gender is decided by the lemma + agreement, not by the أفعال shape. أَنْهَار is the plural of نَهْر 'river', NOT نَهَار 'daytime' — see the ن ه ر quarantine in ../rules/homograph-quarantines.json.

patterns.test: ḍamma-ḍamma or ḍamma-fatḥa internal vocalisation, no external suffix.
patterns.singular_hint: فُعُل pluralises فَعُول/فِعَال singulars (رَسُول ← رُسُل); فُعَل pluralises فُعْلَة (غُرْفَة ← غُرَف).
patterns.guard: رُسُل is a broken plural of رَسُول and refers to rational beings, yet — being a broken (not sound-masculine) plural — it may take feminine-singular agreement on its verb/adjective. Decide agreement from the agreement cue, not from the referent's natural sex.

patterns.test: ـَاء (alif + hamza) terminal; pluralises adjectival فَعِيل singulars denoting rational masculine referents.
patterns.singular_hint: فَعِيل ← فُعَلَاء (شَهِيد ← شُهَدَاء, نَبِيّ ← أَنْبِيَاء via أَفْعِلَاء).
patterns.guard: The terminal ـَاء is a feminine/plural marker here, NOT a radical hamza. Do not certify a hamza-final root from this surface; check the seat per ../rules/hamza-gates.json before reading a third radical hamza.

patterns.test: the 'broken plural of plenty' (ṣīghat muntahā al-jumūʿ): a long ā after the second radical and a kasra before the last. Diptote — no tanwīn, oblique takes fatḥa.
patterns.singular_hint: quadriliteral / augmented singulars (مَسْجِد ← مَسَاجِد, رِسَالَة ← رَسَائِل).
patterns.guard: This class is MAMNŪʿ MIN AL-ṢARF (diptote): it refuses tanwīn and takes FATḤA in the oblique (فِي مَسَاجِدَ, not *مَسَاجِدٍ). Authoring an oblique kasra-tanwīn here is a known error — verify the case vowel before glossing case.

gender_cues.test: lemma ends in tā' marbūṭa ـة → feminine by morphology (مَدِينَة, شَجَرَة).
gender_cues.guard: Strong cue but NOT absolute: a few ـة words are masculine (خَلِيفَة 'successor', عَلَّامَة 'great scholar') and take masculine agreement. The marbūṭa is necessary-not-sufficient; confirm against the agreement cue when stakes are high.

gender_cues.test: semantically/conventionally feminine lemma carrying NO ـة: body parts that come in pairs (يَد, عَيْن, أُذُن), and the fixed muʾannath samāʿī set (شَمْس, نَفْس, أَرْض, نَار, رِيح).
gender_cues.guard: Never infer masculine merely because ـة is absent. شَمْس, أَرْض, نَفْس, نَار are feminine and govern feminine verbs/adjectives despite the bare skeleton. This is the single most common over-application of the marbūṭa shortcut.

gender_cues.test: ANY plural of a non-rational noun (broken or sound) takes FEMININE-SINGULAR agreement on its verb, adjective, and demonstrative.
gender_cues.guard: This is grammatical agreement (mā lā yaʿqil), independent of the singular's gender. أَيَّام (m. sg. يَوْم) still takes feminine-singular agreement in the plural. Do not 'correct' a feminine-singular adjective on a non-rational plural to masculine plural.

gender_cues.test: a BROKEN plural — even of rational beings — may take feminine-singular agreement, unlike the sound-masculine plural which takes masculine-plural agreement.
gender_cues.guard: Contrast مُسْلِمُونَ (sound, masc-pl agreement) with رُسُل (broken, may take fem-sg). The plural KIND, not the referent, sets the licensed agreement. Read the verb's gender/number from the text; do not normalise it.

gender_cues.test: ṣīghat muntahā al-jumūʿ and فُعَلَاء/أَفْعِلَاء classes are diptote: no tanwīn, oblique = fatḥa.
gender_cues.gender: n/a (case behaviour, recorded as a cue)
gender_cues.guard: A missing tanwīn here is GRAMMATICAL, not a transcription gap; do not add one. If a gloss asserts the genitive with kasra-tanwīn on a diptote plural, flag it for repair per ../rules/root-decision-rules.json (qamus_entry_needs_repair).

forbidden_shortcuts.pattern: treating presence/absence of ـة as a total decision for gender
forbidden_shortcuts.why: خَلِيفَة (masc, +ة) and شَمْس/أَرْض/نَفْس (fem, −ة) both falsify it. Use the gender_cues together with agreement; the marbūṭa is one signal, not the verdict.

forbidden_shortcuts.pattern: classifying a broken plural (and inferring its singular/root) from the undiacritized consonant skeleton
forbidden_shortcuts.why: أَنْهَار vs نَهَار, أَيْمَان vs إِيمَان collapse without harakāt/seat. The plural pattern proposes a class only after the diacritics are read; root certification still goes through ../rules/root-decision-rules.json and the hamza seats through ../rules/hamza-gates.json.

forbidden_shortcuts.pattern: counting the sound-plural suffix ـُونَ/ـِينَ/ـَات as part of the triliteral root
forbidden_shortcuts.why: مُسْلِمُون: the ـُون is inflection; the root is س ل م. Mistaking the suffix for a radical fabricates a quadriliteral root and a wrong gloss. norm() also erases the case vowel, so it cannot decide this — see norm_never_certifies in ../rules/root-decision-rules.json.

forbidden_shortcuts.pattern: forcing masculine-plural agreement on every plural of rational beings
forbidden_shortcuts.why: Only the SOUND masculine plural mandates masc-pl agreement; broken plurals of rational beings (رُسُل, أَنْبِيَاء) license feminine-singular agreement. Decide from the plural kind, read agreement off the verse, never normalise it.

forbidden_shortcuts.pattern: auto-glossing any أَفْعَال surface by its commonest member
forbidden_shortcuts.why: أَيْمَان could be 'oaths' (ي م ن) and أَنْهَار 'rivers' (ن ه ر) — both are أفعال but their singulars/roots differ. Defer to the homograph quarantines and certify the singular root before glossing the plural.
